label: Teutoburg Forest
sub: Battle of Teutoburg Forest (9 CE) — Arminius destroys three Roman legions, halting Roman expansion into Germania

label: Aachen (Carolingian Capital)
sub: Capital of Charlemagne's Frankish Empire (768–814); site of coronation of Holy Roman Emperors until 1531

label: Wittenberg — Reformation
sub: Martin Luther posts 95 Theses on Castle Church door (October 31, 1517), sparking the Protestant Reformation

label: Worms — Diet of Worms
sub: Diet of Worms (1521): Luther refuses to recant before Emperor Charles V — 'Here I stand, I can do no other'

label: Augsburg — Peace 1555
sub: Peace of Augsburg (1555): first legal recognition of Lutheranism in HRE — 'cuius regio, eius religio' principle

label: Münster — Peace of Westphalia
sub: Peace of Westphalia (1648) ends Thirty Years War; establishes modern nation-state system and German religious pluralism

label: Potsdam — Frederick the Great
sub: Sans-Souci Palace: residence of Frederick II (the Great) of Prussia, who ruled 1740–1786; site of 1945 Potsdam Conference

label: Leipzig — Battle of Nations
sub: Battle of Leipzig (October 1813): Napoleon decisively defeated by Coalition — largest land battle in European history until WWI

label: Frankfurt — 1848 Parliament
sub: Frankfurt Parliament (Paulskirchenparlament, 1848): first freely elected all-German assembly; attempt at liberal unification fails

label: German Empire Proclaimed 1871
sub: German Empire proclaimed in Hall of Mirrors, Versailles (January 18, 1871) after Franco-Prussian War victory; Bismarck as Chancellor

label: Berlin — Reichstag/Bundestag
sub: Germany's parliament since 1894; burned 1933 (enabling Hitler's emergency powers); remodeled by Norman Foster 1999 as reunified Bundestag

label: Kiel — Sailors' Mutiny 1918
sub: Kiel Mutiny (November 3, 1918): German sailors refuse suicidal naval battle, triggering German Revolution and Kaiser Wilhelm II's abdication

label: Weimar — Republic Founded
sub: Weimar Republic's National Assembly convenes (January 1919); Germany's first democratic constitution adopted in this cultural capital

label: Munich — Beer Hall Putsch
sub: Beer Hall Putsch (November 8–9, 1923): Hitler's failed coup attempt; 16 Nazis and 4 police killed; Hitler imprisoned and writes Mein Kampf

label: Nuremberg — NSDAP Rallies
sub: Annual Nazi Party rallies 1933–1938; Nuremberg Laws (1935) enacted here stripping Jews of citizenship; Nuremberg Trials 1945–1946

label: Wannsee — Holocaust Conference
sub: Wannsee Conference (January 20, 1942): senior Nazi officials coordinate the 'Final Solution' — systematic murder of European Jews

label: Brandenburg Gate
sub: Symbol of German division (1961–1989) and reunification; Reagan's 'tear down this wall' speech 1987; Wall falls November 9, 1989

label: Checkpoint Charlie
sub: Main Allied crossing point through Berlin Wall (1961–1989); site of October 1961 US–USSR tank standoff; demolished June 1990

label: Berlin Wall (East Side Gallery)
sub: 1,316 km of inner German border built August 1961; longest preserved section at East Side Gallery; last major section demolished 1990

label: Bonn — West German Capital
sub: Capital of FRG 1949–1991; seat of Bundestag, Federal Chancellery; 'provisional capital' that became permanent until reunification

label: Hamburg — Hanseatic Port
sub: Key member of Hanseatic League (13th–17th centuries); Germany's largest port; still styled 'Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg'

label: Heidelberg — Oldest University
sub: Ruprecht-Karls-Universität (founded 1386), Germany's oldest university; center of German Romanticism and academic tradition

label: Dresden — Allied Bombing 1945
sub: Dresden firebombed February 13–15, 1945; approx. 25,000 killed (German commission 2010); Frauenkirche rebuilt and reconsecrated 2005

label: Cologne — Cathedral & Rhineland
sub: Cologne Cathedral (begun 1248, completed 1880): UNESCO site; Rhineland occupied by France post-WWI (1919–1930) and post-WWII

label: Erfurt — SPD & Luther
sub: Augustine monastery where Luther became a monk (1505); SPD Erfurt Programme adopted 1891 establishing Marxist platform

label: Regensburg — Imperial Diet
sub: Regensburg hosted the Perpetual Imperial Diet (Immerwährender Reichstag) 1663–1806 — permanent parliament of the Holy Roman Empire

label: Wilhelmshaven — Naval Base
sub: Germany's main naval base; Kaiser Wilhelm II's High Seas Fleet based here during WWI; site of 1918 Sailors' Mutiny origins; remains major Bundeswehr naval base

label: Buchenwald — Concentration Camp
sub: Nazi concentration camp near Weimar (1937–1945): over 56,000 prisoners killed; among first camps liberated by US Army (April 11, 1945)

label: Berlin — Holocaust Memorial
sub: Memorial to Murdered Jews of Europe (opened May 2005): 2,711 concrete stelae; adjacent to Brandenburg Gate; architect Peter Eisenman

label: Bonn — Federal Chancellery
sub: Site of reunification diplomacy (1989–1990) and Kohl government 1982–1998; German government's operational seat until full Berlin move 1999

label: East Berlin — Alexanderplatz Demo
sub: November 4, 1989: 500,000 people rally at Alexanderplatz — largest demonstration in GDR history; Wall falls 5 days later
